focus: Depth Guard Enforcement - Part 2: Verify depth-guard-lint.sh test coverage
executive_summary: tests/depth-guard-lint.sh exists but is not invoked by CI; there are no automated fixtures asserting pass/fail behavior.
Lint enforces leaf/commander invariants via brittle substring checks (format/casing/spacing sensitive) and all explicit failures exit 1 (no per-check exit codes).
Depth enforcement is mostly static (phrase presence + frontmatter tool bans). There is no explicit depth=2 (Squad Lead) prompt enforcement test; runtime depth_config fields are not validated.

key_findings: Current lint checks: worker frontmatter forbids task/ask_user; worker required phrases; worker forbids can_launch=true; commander required phrases; commander includes worker prompt lock phrases.
CI does not invoke depth-guard-lint.sh; smoke-test.sh does not invoke it.
Brittleness: forbidden-tools check is literal substring ('  - task'); commander checks include fragile prose tokens (e.g., gpt-5.5, Premium Model Policy).
gaps: No automated negative/positive fixtures (forbidden tool, missing invariant, can_launch=true).
stampede-merger.agent.md is not linted though listed as a core agent.
Leaf tools read_agent/list_agents are not explicitly forbidden in worker tools.
recommended_changes: Use regex/YAML parsing for frontmatter tools to avoid indentation sensitivity.
Add worker invariant 'DO NOT use the task tool' to required_worker_phrases (align with commander Worker Depth Lock block).

key_findings: No numeric depth-level parsing exists in depth-guard-lint.sh; it validates static phrases and tool bans.
Runtime depth_budget JSON is partially validated by smoke-test.sh, not by lint.
Commander Worker Depth Lock enforcement is only validated by phrase presence inside commander markdown, not by verifying real prompt injection into generated child prompts.
gaps: No depth=2 (Squad Lead) prompt enforcement test.
No tests validate depth_config.current_depth/max_depth/can_launch Context Capsule fields.
can_launch detection misses variants (no-space, single quotes, case variants).
recommended_changes: Add fixture tests for malformed frontmatter and missing files with clear lint errors (no tracebacks).
Add checks for stable JSON-shape tokens (e.g., '"model_policy": "premium"') rather than prose-only tokens.

key_findings: Banned tools enforced: task/ask_user in worker frontmatter; can_launch=true in worker body.
No unit tests assert banned-tool detection; CI does not run lint.
Edge cases: case/format variants can bypass substring checks; single-quote can_launch not covered.
gaps: No tests asserting that task-like strings in markdown body are ignored (current behavior only scans frontmatter).
recommended_changes: Extend forbidden worker tools list to include read_agent/list_agents.
Use anchored multiline regexes to match YAML list items precisely; optionally case-normalize.

key_findings: All explicit failures raise SystemExit -> exit code 1 (no per-check codes).
Unhandled parsing errors (missing --- separators) or missing files cause tracebacks with non-zero exit but poor diagnostics.
gaps: No tests assert exit codes for failure cases.
No per-category exit codes for easier CI diagnosis.
recommended_changes: Add fixture tests that assert non-zero exit per failure type.
Optionally add distinct exit codes per category.
Wrap parsing/file reads with explicit error messages to avoid tracebacks.

key_findings: Messages identify invariant/tool but do not include file path/line numbers.
gaps: No stable message contract for tests.
recommended_changes: Include file name in every failure message.
Optionally add line numbers/context excerpts; standardize format (e.g., 'ERROR: file: detail').
Add message-pattern tests (assert substrings, not full lines).

high_priority_actions.action: Run Depth Guard lint in CI
high_priority_actions.rationale: Without CI invocation, regressions in Depth Guard invariants can merge silently.

high_priority_actions.details: Create minimal fixtures that intentionally violate each rule and assert non-zero exit + message contains key token.
high_priority_actions.rationale: Ensures each lint rule is reachable and prevents accidental weakening.

high_priority_actions.details: Replace split/substrings with YAML parsing or anchored multiline regex; emit clear errors for malformed frontmatter.
high_priority_actions.rationale: Current approach is brittle and can produce false negatives or tracebacks.

required_worker_phrases: You MUST fully complete one task before claiming the next
DO NOT attempt to spawn sub-agents
required_commander_phrases_examples: Do not satisfy metaswarm by launching a single wrapped `swarm-command` agent

recommended_additional_invariants: Worker: add 'DO NOT use the task tool'
Commander: add stable JSON-shape tokens like '"status": "partial"', '"banned_child_models"', '"premium_model_pool"'
